title: 5 Biblical Tests for God’s Voice
hook: Is that God or your anxiety? Use these 5 tests.
script: Jesus said, My sheep hear my voice (John 10:27). To discern quickly: One, does this align with Scripture? God never contradicts His Word (2 Tim 3:16). Two, does it look like Jesus—love, joy, peace, patience (Gal 5:22-23)? Three, do you sense peace ruling, not panic pushing (Col 3:15)? Four, do wise believers affirm it (Prov 11:14)? Five, wait and watch for fruit; good trees show over time (Matt 7:16). If it fails, release it. If it passes, obey in faith. Simple prayer: Lord, tune my ears to You. Replay this the next time anxiety speaks loud.

title: Real-Life Discernment: The Rushy Offer
hook: You may be overthinking this—try the five tests in real life.
script: Picture this: a flashy offer lands in your inbox. Decide today. Don’t tell anyone. Sounds urgent. Test it. Scripture: does it require cutting corners? God won’t endorse dishonesty (2 Tim 3:16). Character: will it grow love, patience, self-control—or pride and envy (Gal 5:22-23)? Peace: is your soul calm or pressured (Col 3:15)? Counsel: what do your trusted, godly friends say (Prov 11:14)? Fruit: imagine six months—more life or more regret (Matt 7:16)? If it’s God, urgency bows to peace, and truth stands. If not, wait. The Shepherd leads; He never shoves.

title: 30-Second Prayer to Hear God Clearly
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this and pray with me.
script: Jesus, You said, My sheep hear My voice (John 10:27). I quiet my anxious thoughts. Speak through Your Word; do not let me follow anything You have not written (2 Tim 3:16). Form Christlike fruit in me—love, joy, peace, patience (Gal 5:22-23). Let Your peace, not pressure, rule in my heart (Col 3:15). Confirm Your leading through wise counsel (Prov 11:14). And over time, make the fruit unmistakably good (Matt 7:16). Journal this: What verse guides this choice? What fruit do I expect in three months? Lord, I will wait and obey the light I have.
